channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Deputada pede afastamento de Gonet da PGR por omissões no caso Master
item.description: Federal Deputy Carol de Toni (PL-SC) filed a representation with the CNMP requesting an investigation into the conduct of Attorney General Paulo Gonet over alleged omissions in the Master case. She also sent a recommendation to President Lula (PT) to evaluate Gonet's continued tenure. The move comes amid growing criticism of the PGR's handling of investigations involving political figures.

item.title: Governo Lula lança ECA Digital para ampliar proteçăo ŕs crianças na internet
item.description: The Lula government launched the ECA Digital, a statute expanding online protection for children and adolescents, requiring prior judicial authorization to monetize content involving minors, strengthening age verification, and banning personalized ads and compulsive-use features. The decree also establishes a National Center for Child and Adolescent Protection linked to the Federal Police and structures the ANPD to regulate and enforce compliance with the new law.

item.title: “Janja não é modelo de primeira-dama tradicional, é liderança”, diz PT
item.description: Edinho Silva, national president of the Workers' Party (PT), states that Janja Lula da Silva does not fit the traditional first-lady model but is a political leader with a militant background in the party since adolescence. He emphasizes she will play an important role in Lula's 2026 re-election campaign, though not in formal campaign coordination, maintaining her own agenda and continuing to advocate for causes such as animal welfare.

item.title: Lula assina decretos que regulamentam ECA Digital; veja principais pontos
item.description: President Lula signed three decrees to regulate the Digital ECA, which requires social media platforms to prevent children and adolescents from accessing harmful content. The decrees ban manipulative practices such as infinite scrolling, autoplay videos, and reward systems in games, while also establishing a PF reporting center and restructuring the ANPD.

item.title: OAB-RJ vê perigo de judicialização em MP para enfrentar alta de petróleo
item.description: The OAB-RJ warns of the risk of judicialization of Provisional Measure 1.340, which reintroduces an export tax on crude oil and diesel to stabilize fuel prices. The association argues that the measure, similar to MP 1163 of 2023 — already ruled as revenue-raising by the TRF2 — may violate constitutionality by having a fiscal rather than regulatory purpose, creating legal uncertainty and compliance costs for the sector.

item.title: Justiça retoma benefícios de Bolsonaro e mantém carros oficiais mesmo com ex-presidente na Papudinha
item.description: The TRF-6 reinstated Jair Bolsonaro's lifelong benefits, including staff, official vehicles, drivers, and security, even while he is imprisoned at the Papudinha facility, overturning a prior decision that had suspended them due to incompatibility with closed regime. The court ruled that the law does not condition such benefits on the former president's freedom and that services extend beyond personal security to include advisory and logistical support. Expenses for this structure have exceeded R$ 4 million since 2023.

item.title: “É como se tivéssemos um papa pedófilo”, diz Zema sobre STF
item.description: Governor of Minas Gerais, Romeu Zema (Novo), compared the actions of the Brazilian Supreme Federal Court (STF) to 'a pedophile pope' during an agribusiness event in Belo Horizonte. He criticized the STF for no longer serving as a reference of authority and dignity, suggesting its decisions are undermining the Court’s credibility. The remark sparked controversy due to its extreme and offensive analogy.

item.title: Vereador do PT aciona PGR após Valdemar dizer que espera ajuda de Trump para Flávio
item.description: Belo Horizonte city councilor Pedro Rousseff (PT) filed a representation with the Attorney General's Office (PGR) requesting an investigation after PL national president Valdemar Costa Neto said he expects support from Donald Trump for Flávio Bolsonaro's candidacy. Rousseff alleges potential illegal foreign interference in Brazil's elections, which violates the Constitution's ban on parties receiving foreign resources or submitting to foreign governments. The request includes summoning Valdemar for clarification and probing for signs of electoral law violations.

item.title: PT delineia estratégia para tentar massacrar Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: The Workers' Party (PT) has outlined an aggressive strategy to attack Flávio Bolsonaro in the 2026 elections, focusing on allegations of illicit enrichment via 'rachadinhas', subservience to U.S. interests under Donald Trump, and the preservation of historical privileges. The party also plans to reframe the Banco Master scandal, linking Daniel Vorcaro to Jair Bolsonaro’s administration and Roberto Campos Neto, while José Dirceu ruled out any tone of conciliation, stating there will be no 'Lula peace and love' in 2026.

item.title: Ao vivo: Governo Lula regulamenta o ECA Digital
item.description: The Lula government regulated the Digital Child and Adolescent Statute (ECA Digital), Law No. 15.211/2025, with three decrees signed by the president on March 18, 2026, establishing rules for digital platforms, creating a National Center for the Protection of Children and Adolescents linked to the Federal Police, and defining the ANPD’s role in oversight. The law bans loot boxes in youth-oriented games, combats manipulative design, and requires rapid platform responses to grooming, harassment, and sexual exploitation, promoting shared responsibility among the state, companies, and families.

item.title: Veneziano agradece a Renan Filho pelo trabalho no Ministério dos Transportes
item.description: Senator Veneziano Vital do Rêgo (MDB-PB) praised Transport Minister Renan Filho for his effective and results-driven leadership, citing the restart of the BR-230 duplication project in Paraíba as a key achievement. He criticized the previous administration (until 2022) for underfunding, stalled projects, and lack of initiative, noting that progress only resumed under President Lula’s government and through parliamentary advocacy, including a R$157 million amendment led by Marcelo Castro (MDB-PI). Veneziano emphasized the project’s economic and safety benefits for Campina Grande and the state’s interior.

item.title: Zema compara STF a papa pedófilo e diz que tribunal 'está aprontando'
item.description: Minas Gerais Governor and presidential pre-candidate Romeu Zema (Novo) compared the actions of the Supreme Federal Court (STF) to those of a pedophile pope, stating the tribunal 'is up to something'. The remarks were made on Wednesday (18) and reiterate his frequent criticisms of the Judiciary.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro publica vídeo que associa Lula e Lulinha às fraudes do INSS
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro published an AI-generated video on social media associating President Lula and his son Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha) to an INSS fraud scheme involving businessman Antônio Camilo Antunes, known as 'Careca do INSS'. The post follows a PT offensive labeling him a candidate 'marked by denunciations and investigations', amid recent polls showing a technical tie between Flávio Bolsonaro and Lula in simulated 2026 presidential runoff scenarios.

item.title: Brent passa de US$ 107 o barril e pressiona inflação global
item.description: Brent crude surpassed $107 per barrel due to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and supply constraints, driving up global inflation and increasing fuel and freight costs. In Brazil, the impact affects Petrobras' pricing policy, heightens domestic volatility, and prompted the government to announce measures to curb diesel inflation, including Lula’s package estimated at R$ 30 billion.

item.title: Deus me livre de ser julgada por juiz que não seja imparcial e ético, diz Cármen Lúcia
item.description: TSE President Minister Cármen Lúcia stated that Brazilian citizens must be judged by competent, impartial, and ethical magistrates. She emphasized the importance of judicial impartiality for public trust in institutions.

item.title: Empresários “se equivocam” ao serem contra fim da escala 6 x 1, diz PT
item.description: National PT president Edinho Silva defended ending the 6x1 work schedule as a social justice measure, criticizing business leaders who oppose it. He argued that reducing working hours is crucial to give workers—especially women—more time for family, education, and mental health. Edinho also noted a constitutional amendment proposal (PEC) is advancing in Congress and that the Planalto plans to send an urgency bill (PL) after Carnival for fast-track voting.

item.title: Polarização dificulta comunicação do governo Lula, diz PT
item.description: PT national president Edinho Silva said political polarization hinders the government's ability to communicate its achievements to voters, despite policies like expanding income tax exemption for those earning up to R$ 5,000 and the Pé-de-Meia student aid program. He warned communication will be a key obstacle in the 2026 electoral campaign and cited ongoing federal works as examples of government actions not being properly perceived by the public.

item.title: Marqueteiro de Ratinho Jr. ganha prêmio por campanha para partido mexicano
item.description: Jorge Gerez, political strategist for Paraná Governor Ratinho Júnior (PSD), received an award for a campaign he designed for Mexico’s National Action Party (PAN), a center-right political party. The recognition highlights his international work in political communication.

item.title: Crece la presión para que Bolsonaro regrese a su casa para cumplir la pena en prisión domiciliaria
item.description: Lawyers for former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro, convicted to 27 years in prison for coup-related charges, have formally requested that he serve the remainder of his sentence under house arrest at his Brasília residence, citing his current hospitalization in intensive care for acute pneumonia. Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, acting as his father’s lawyer, met with Judge Alexandre de Moraes to strengthen the plea, which had previously been denied. Medical reports indicate improvement, with a possible transfer to a regular ward before the weekend.

item.title: PL define apoio a Sergio Moro na disputa pelo governo do Paraná
item.description: The Paraná state directory of the PL party has declared its support for Sergio Moro's pre-candidacy for governor, breaking with Ratinho Jr. (PSD), who is expected to run for president in 2026. The party has not yet decided under which party Moro will run, though he is considering switching to the PL, and plans to nominate Filipe Barros for Senate on the same ticket. The support reflects Ratinho Jr.'s resistance to abandoning his presidential ambitions, despite PL pressure.

item.title: PL confirma apoio à candidatura de Sergio Moro no Paraná
item.description: The Liberal Party (PL) has confirmed its support for a potential Sergio Moro candidacy for governor of Paraná, reinforcing the invitation for him to join the party. The agreement aims to secure a political platform for Flávio Bolsonaro in the state, given Ratinho Jr.'s presidential pre-candidacy and Moro's internal resistance within União Brasil. Moro is expected to meet with União Brasil leadership this week to finalize his party affiliation.

item.title: Caiado diz que Goiás será parceiro dos EUA, e não só fornecedor
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) stated that the cooperation agreement on critical minerals and rare earths with the United States shifts the state from being merely a raw material supplier to a strategic partner. The memorandum, signed in São Paulo, emphasizes research, training, transparent regulation, and incentives for local production, job creation, and technology transfer, which Caiado called the most significant geoeconomic agreement in Goiás’ history.

item.title: AtlasIntel aponta favoritismo de Rafael Fonteles (PT) no Piauí; veja os números
item.description: Governor Rafael Fonteles (PT) leads the Piauí gubernatorial race with 56-57.7% voting intention, securing a first-round victory according to AtlasIntel. In the Senate race, Marcelo Castro (MDB) leads with 24.5%, followed by a technical tie between Júlio César (PSD) and Ciro Nogueira (PP), both around 18%. The survey interviewed 1,208 people between March 11-15, with a 3-point margin of error.

item.title: As intenções de voto de Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro no Piauí, segundo AtlasIntel
item.description: Lula leads with 61.9% voting intentions in the first round in Piauí, followed by Flávio Bolsonaro with 22.2%, according to an AtlasIntel poll conducted from March 11–15. In second-round scenarios, Lula maintains an absolute lead over various candidates, including Flávio Bolsonaro (67% to 25%).

item.title: Roberta Luchsinger 'entrou em pânico' com envelope para Lulinha por medo de exposição, diz defesa
item.description: The defense of Roberta Luchsinger claimed she panicked upon learning that the Federal Police had seized an envelope with the name of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha) in the locker of businessman Antônio Camilo, fearing the president’s son’s name would be politically exploited, even without knowing the envelope’s contents. According to lawyer Bruno Salles, her instruction to discard phones was an impulsive crisis management move, not an attempt to hide crimes, as Roberta only later learned the envelope contained unused football tickets.

item.title: Governo muda orientação e instrui aliados a partir para ataque contra Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: The federal government has shifted its strategy, instructing allies to launch an offensive against Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, now seen as Lula's main rival in the 2026 elections. The move follows a Datafolha poll showing Flávio tied with Lula in a potential second-round runoff, undermining earlier hopes that he could be marginalized. Lula's allies now prefer to run against Flávio rather than São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas, viewed as a Bolsonaro backup plan.

item.title: Ala econômica do governo diz não ter pressa em anunciar novos diretores do BC
item.description: Lula's economic team said there is no rush to appoint two new directors to the Central Bank, citing political instability from the Banco Master scandal and the INSS CPI leaks, including those involving Fábio Luís Lula da Silva. The appointment is being delayed to avoid worsening the political climate and giving ammunition to the opposition, with concerns over further leaks and Senate resistance, especially after the controversial nomination of Jorge Messias to the STF.

item.title: Festa de Dirceu reúne ministros e ex-advogado de Vorcaro em Brasília
item.description: Former Minister José Dirceu hosted a birthday celebration in Brasília for his 80th anniversary, attended by Lula government ministers, congressmembers, and political allies. Notable attendees included Geraldo Alckmin, Camilo Santana, Gleisi Hoffmann, Sonia Guajajara, and Pierpaolo Bottini, former lawyer for banker Daniel Vorcaro. Dirceu used the event to unveil his campaign jingle and confirm his bid for a federal deputy seat in São Paulo in the 2026 elections.

item.title: “Igualdade de gênero está nas leis, não na vida”, afirma ministra Cármen Lúcia
item.description: TSE President Minister Cármen Lúcia warned that gender equality exists in laws but not in political practice in Brazil, citing gender-based political violence, disinformation, and frauds against female quotas as ongoing barriers to women's full electoral participation. She noted that sexist attacks on women differ from those against men, often targeting families and causing self-withdrawal, and that digital technologies amplify these hate discourses.

item.title: Câmara rechaça tentativa de enquadrar desfile que homenageou Lula como intolerância religiosa
item.description: The Municipal Chamber of Niterói rejected by a vote of 10 to 6 a motion of repudiation against the Acadêmicos de Niterói samba school for its parade honoring President Lula, which was accused of religious intolerance. The motion, introduced by councilman Allan Lyra (PL), criticized a float featuring costumes resembling canned goods, claiming it mocked evangelical families. Most council members ruled the display was protected under artistic freedom and social critique, despite the school's subsequent relegation to the Série Ouro division.

item.title: Aumenta chance de Alexandre de Moraes liberar Jair Bolsonaro para a prisão domiciliar
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes is considering transferring Jair Bolsonaro to house arrest due to his deteriorating health, including bilateral bacterial pneumonia and hospitalization at DF Star. Despite pressure from Bolsonaro's defense and allies, Moraes is not rushing the decision, weighing political context and recent attacks on the STF following leaked messages with Daniel Vorcaro. The move could serve as a gesture of appeasement ahead of a potential right-wing congressional surge in 2027.

item.title: Advogado de Roberta Luchsinger diz que empresária não faz ideia de quem seja 'filho do rapaz' que aparece em mensagens
item.description: The lawyer for Roberta Luchsinger stated she has no idea who the 'son of the guy' is, referenced in messages from lobbyist Careca do INSS, tied to R$1.5 million in payments from his companies to hers. Messages and testimonies suggest the funds may have been directed to Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), son of President Lula, though his lawyer denies any receipt. There are suspicions the payments were meant to facilitate the sale of cannabidiol products from World Cannabis to the Ministry of Health.

item.title: Pesquisas animam e PL confia em vitória de Flávio Bolsonaro ‘se não errar muito’
item.description: Recent polls show Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) technically tied with Lula (PT) in the 2026 presidential runoff, with 41% according to Quaest and 43% according to Datafolha. The PL believes he can win if the campaign avoids major mistakes, emphasizing the need for careful state-level coalition-building, especially in the Northeast and Minas Gerais, to broaden his appeal among centrist voters.

item.title: Câmara terá “palavra final” sobre cassar deputados do PL, diz Motta
item.description: Chamber of Deputies President Hugo Motta stated that the House will have the 'final word' on the mandate loss of PL deputies convicted by the STF for embezzlement of parliamentary amendments, after the case becomes final and unappealable. The process will follow the CCJ review and then a plenary vote, respecting internal rules. Deputies Josimar Maranhãozinho, Pastor Gil, and Bosco Costa were unanimously convicted by the STF’s 1st Chamber for receiving bribes equivalent to 25% of the amendment funds directed to the municipality of São José de Ribamar.

item.title: O cálculo político por trás do novo pedido de domiciliar de Bolsonaro
item.description: Bolsonaro's defense has intensified its request for house arrest based on worsening health conditions, including pneumonia and bronchoaspiration, following a meeting with Minister Alexandre de Moraes. Allies suggest a potential ruling between June and July, ahead of the 2026 party conventions, aims to reduce the electoral mobilization potential of the victimization narrative and 'cool' the public debate. However, experts stress the decision will depend on clinical evolution, and the political impact will persist regardless of the judicial outcome.

item.title: Vanderlei Luxemburgo ganha apoio do Podemos para concorrer ao Senado de Tocantins
item.description: Vanderlei Luxemburgo received support from the Podemos party to run for the Senate in Tocantins in the 2026 elections. National party president Renata Abreu emphasized his preparation, history, and commitment to the state's development. Luxemburgo, who resides and owns property in Tocantins, previously attempted a Senate run in 2022 under the PSB but did not receive party backing at the time.

item.title: Brasil supera EUA em ranking mundial de democracia pela 1ª vez
item.description: Brazil surpassed the United States in the global democracy ranking for the first time in 2026, according to the V-Dem Institute's Democracy Report from the University of Gothenburg. The result reflects improvements in political participation, press freedom, and the rule of law in Brazil, while the U.S. showed setbacks in several democratic dimensions.

item.title: Por que a mensagem de Lula com a indicação de Messias para o STF não deve chegar ao Congresso antes de abril
item.description: The nomination of Jorge Messias to the STF by the Lula government is not expected to reach Congress before April, despite his favorable standing among senators. Senator Weverton Rocha, appointed as rapporteur, remains in Maranhão and is not expected to return to Brasília in March. Messias has spoken with most senators but still seeks a meeting with the Congress president, while figures like Flávio Bolsonaro and Eduardo Girão have no hope of engagement.

item.title: João Campos fecha chapa mais à esquerda para governo de Pernambuco, com Marília Arraes e Humberto Costa
item.description: João Campos (PSB), mayor of Recife, closed agreements with Humberto Costa (PT) and Marília Arraes (PDT) to form his gubernatorial ticket in Pernambuco, positioning the campaign further to the left and aligned with Lula. The vice-presidential slot remains open, with Silvio Costa Filho (Republicanos-PE) as the leading candidate, as the alliance aims to counter Governor Raquel Lyra (PSD), who seeks Lula’s neutrality and to absorb União Brasil and PP.

item.title: Delivery entra no radar político
item.description: A Quaest poll commissioned by the ANR shows 71% of Brazilians reject the Lula government's proposal to set a minimum fee for app-based deliveries, with 78% believing it would increase order costs. Rejection is particularly strong among right-wing voters (97%) and also among independents (83%), a decisive group in the 2026 elections, suggesting the measure could generate significant political backlash due to its perceived regressive impact and cost burden on low-income populations.

item.title: ONGs recorrem à ONU para evitar que governo brasileiro sancione PL Antifacção
item.description: Human rights NGOs Conectas Direitos Humanos and Rede Justiça Criminal appealed to the UN to urge the Lula government not to fully sanction the Anti-Faction Bill, arguing it threatens judicial guarantees and represents a human rights regression, including criminalizing families of inmates, ending prison assistance, and banning voting for pretrial detainees. They requested the UN pressure the government to conduct a social, legislative, and budgetary impact analysis before the expected sanction on March 19, 2026.

item.title: Governo libera R$ 1,3 bi para reconstrução em MG após chuva
item.description: The federal government has allocated R$ 1.3 billion for response and reconstruction efforts in areas affected by intense rainfall, primarily in Minas Gerais, through Provisional Measure No. 1.342/2026 signed by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. Funds will support social assistance (R$ 5 million for SUAS), housing (R$ 500 million for FAR, enabling 2,500 housing units), and credit access (R$ 300 million for FGO and R$ 500 million for climate mitigation and adaptation), coordinated by the Ministries of Social Development and Cities, with financial management by the Ministry of Finance.

item.title: A grande festa de vinhos com chef estrelado, DJs e convites a 400 dólares
item.description: The 2026 harvest festival at Bodega Garzón in Uruguay attracted around 400 guests, mostly Brazilians, for an exclusive experience featuring celebrity chef Francis Mallmann, premium wines, international DJs, and a 150-meter imperial table. Tickets priced at R$ 2.775 (about US$ 400) sold out in two days, underscoring the demand for luxury events blending wine culture, haute cuisine, and elite social experiences.

item.title: Daniel Vilela lidera disputa pelo governo de Goiás, diz pesquisa
item.description: Vice-governor Daniel Vilela (MDB) leads the race for governor of Goiás in all first-round scenarios, with 34% to 36% support, followed by Marconi Perillo (PSDB) at 24% to 26%. In second-round simulations, Vilela defeats all opponents comfortably and has the lowest rejection rate among major candidates at 24%.

item.title: Motta diz que Câmara está “atenta” a alta dos combustíveis
item.description: Chamber of Deputies President Hugo Motta stated that the House is attentive to rising fuel prices and will seek joint solutions with the government to prevent harm to the population, emphasizing that the increase stems from external factors like the Iran war, which disrupts global oil chains and impacts Brazil’s road-dependent logistics. He stressed the need for price stability and dialogue with truckers, the government, and the Senate to avoid national economic imbalances. The Lula administration previously announced a diesel relief package involving tax cuts and subsidies, estimated to cost R$ 30 billion by end-2026.

item.title: PL fecha apoio a pré-candidatura de Sergio Moro, e senador deve ser palanque para Flávio no Paraná
item.description: The Liberal Party (PL) announced its support for Sergio Moro’s pre-candidacy for governor of Paraná, solving the issue of lacking a electoral base for Flávio Bolsonaro in the state. PL president Valdemar Costa Neto confirmed the support but noted Moro must decide whether to stay in União Brasil or join the PL. The move proceeds despite internal PP resistance in Paraná, which vetoed Moro in December, and Valdemar denied this constitutes a break with Ratinho Junior, who is running for president.

item.title: Câmara retoma votações nesta quarta
item.description: The Chamber of Deputies resumes voting this Wednesday (18), with emphasis on PLP 281/19, which establishes Brazil’s new bank resolution regime to ensure financial system stability during crises. Of the 18 agenda items, several are ready for vote — including bills on pensions, security, education, ADHD rights, domestic violence, and racism — while others await urgency approval, such as mandating philosophy and sociology in basic education and regulating digital market competition.

item.title: PL define apoio a Moro para o Governo do Paraná
item.description: The PL announced its support for Senator Sergio Moro's (União Brasil) candidacy for Governor of Paraná, aiming to create a platform for Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL) presidential campaign in the state. The decision followed a meeting between Moro and the party leadership, which leads in voter intention polls.

item.title: As conversas do primeiro encontro de Haddad com o núcleo duro de sua campanha em SP
item.description: Fernando Haddad met with the core team of his São Paulo gubernatorial campaign, including campaign strategist Otávio Antunes, deputy Jilmar Tatto, chief of staff Laio Moraes, and deputy Carlos Zarattini. Haddad acknowledged he had resisted the candidacy but now accepts the mission given by President Lula, insisting that belief in victory is essential. Attendees criticized Tarcísio de Freitas’s administration for 'privatizations and failures in education and tolls,' calling the current government 'very bad,' yet expressed confidence that defeating him is possible.

item.title: Eleições municipais na França: Macron alerta para alianças com partidos 'extremos'
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron warned against forming alliances with parties deemed 'extremes' in the upcoming municipal elections runoff in France. Although his Renaissance party had limited presence in the campaign, Macron stressed the need to avoid pacts with radical forces that threaten democratic stability.
